thread.title: UDF Opposes Judicial Commission for Munambam Land Issue, Calls for Quick Resolution | Thiruvananthapuram News - Times of India
thread.title_full: UDF Opposes Judicial Commission for Munambam Land Issue, Calls for Quick Resolution | Thiruvananthapuram News - Times of India

author: Times Of India
title: UDF Opposes Judicial Commission for Munambam Land Issue, Calls for Quick Resolution | Thiruvananthapuram News - Times of India
text: T'puram: Opposition has opposed the state govt's decision to appoint a judicial commission to study Munambam land issue and recommend a solution within three months. Opposition leader VD Satheesan said the govt was deliberately delaying an issue that could be settled in 10 minutes.\"This delay would only provide an opportunity to communal forces, who are trying to create a division in society. Given that Muslim organizations and Farook College management have offered full support to resolve the issue, the govt could have made a decision and informed the court about it,\" he said.Now, the govt is imposing a unilateral decision. No serious discussion was held with those involved in the protest. The govt's approach to prolong a resolution to the issue is incorrect, he said.He said the govt has not accepted the opposition's demand for an all-party meeting on the issue. By imposing the decision of a judicial commission without consulting anyone, it is clear that the govt has ulterior motives. Kerala is a state with judicial commissions that fail to complete their tasks on time. The govt is consciously denying justice to the poor in Munambam, he added.
